pack.linkedin: I've identified 7 non-REIT dividend stocks with yields exceeding 8%, including one in the energy sector with a 9.2% yield. Using Finbox, I've screened these stocks for dividend sustainability and found that 4 have payout ratios under 60%. A 5-year payout ratio analysis helped me avoid a 20% dividend cut. I'm also considering Vanguard's Dividend Appreciation Index Fund, which has returned 13.4% over the past 10 years. After saving $1,200 in taxes with TaxLossHarvest, I'm now integrating it into my strategy. What's your approach to generating passive income through dividend stocks?
